videos.title: AI didn't make you faster. It just hid the real bottleneck. #Productivity #FutureOfWork
videos.description:
Here's the uncomfortable truth: AI made the building part fast, so now everything around it is the slow part. The meeting to align on a feature. The PRD. The approval loop. There's a manufacturing principle that explains it — kill a bottleneck and it doesn't vanish, it just slides downstream. The work got faster. Our habits didn't.

I broke down the 8 habits worth unlearning (with a prompt kit to find your real bottleneck) here 👉 https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/8-habits-worth-unlearning-i

videos.title: This is the real AI moat — and it's not the models. #anthropic #claude #claudecowork
videos.description:
Anthropic noticed something weird: developers were using their coding tool to sort expense receipts. 10 days later, they'd shipped Claude Cowork. That kind of operational velocity is becoming as big a moat as the models themselves. Want the deeper breakdown on how AI-native teams move this fast? Get my newsletter: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com

My Links 
👉🏻 X: https://x.com/natebjones
👉🏻 TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@nate.b.jones
👉🏻 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nate.b.jones

videos.title: I Was The Only Thing Connecting Claude, ChatGPT, and Codex. So I Built My Replacement.
videos.description:
How to Guide - Build Your Open Engine:
https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/ai-agent-handoffs?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

Your AI agents don't talk to each other, so you have become the human glue moving work between Claude, Codex, and ChatGPT. Open Engine is a shared task queue that lets your agents hand off work, carry the sources, and leave a receipt, without you stuck in the middle.

My Links 🔗
👉🏻 Newsletter: https://natesnewsletter.substa

videos.title: Meet Your Ad Hoc AI Licensing Regime
videos.description:
This week’s AI Weekly Brief looks at the emerging government-limited rollout process for frontier models, from Mythos to GPT-5.6, and why an opaque, customer-by-customer access regime could be bad for everyone. It also covers Claude Tag, open model momentum, CEO-led AI ROI, and the suddenly revived AI infrastructure trade.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI. 
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen: https://po
